title: Username enumeration via timing differences
description: When passwordForUsername returns nil (unknown user), the key exchange aborts immediately at line 1343. When the user exists but password is wrong, it proceeds through ECDSA verification (lines 1352-1369). This timing difference allows username enumeration. Secondary: receiveSendLogin also had no timing equalization on failure path.
recommendation: When username is not found in passwordForUsername, use a dummy hash value instead of aborting, so the key exchange completes with an ECDSA mismatch identical in timing to a wrong password.
